metadata.description: Vision forward-parity fixtures for Sprint V.3+V.4 (#934 ViT-B/16, #935 DenseNet-121, #936 InceptionV3). All entries use random-init weights (weights=None) and synthetic seeded inputs. Tolerance: F32_MATMUL = 1e-3.

fixtures.note: ViT-B/16 ~86M params: patch_embed(589824) + cls_token(768) + pos_embed(151296) + 12 blocks(~85M) + norm(1536) + head(769000). torchvision reference: 86,567,656 parameters.
ViT-B/16 with num_classes=10 must emit [1, 10] logits.
All output logits must be finite (no NaN/Inf) for a zero-init model with constant input.
Two forward passes with fixed weights and fixed input must be bit-identical.
DenseNet-121 (no BN, no conv bias): stem(9408) + blocks(331776+909312+2801664+2129920) + transitions(32768+131072+524288) + classifier(1025000) = ~7.9M. torchvision reference with BN: ~7.97M.
DenseNet-121 with num_classes=10 and 32x32 input (minimum viable spatial size).
All output logits must be finite for a zero-init model with 32x32 input.
Two forward passes with the same model and input must produce bit-identical outputs.

torchvision.models.inception_v3(weights=None, aux_logits=False).eval()(randn(1,3,299,299)) -> [1, 1000]. ferrotorch InceptionV3 is a simplified 3-module variant (stem + 3 InceptionA blocks + AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1,1) + Linear). The 299x299 input (PyTorch canonical) flows through stride-2 stem -> 150x150 -> AdaptiveAvgPool2d -> 1x1. Output shape [1,1000] is invariant of spatial input size.

ferrotorch simplified InceptionV3(1000): stem(864+18432) + 3x InceptionA modules(30208+68864+135168) + Linear(257000) = ~510,536 params. Range 400K-650K accommodates any minor variant. torchvision reference: ~27M (full architecture with all 11 inception modules, factorized convs, aux classifier).
InceptionV3 with num_classes=10 on 299x299 input must emit [1, 10] logits.
All output logits must be finite (no NaN/Inf) for a random-init InceptionV3 with 299x299 input.
Two forward passes with same InceptionV3 weights and same input must produce bit-identical outputs. Uses 32x32 for test speed (AdaptiveAvgPool2d handles any spatial size).
